articles.title: FRAMEWORKS. MORE RISK. MORE PRESSURE TO PROVE TRUST.
META ACQUIRES MOLTBOOK, THE AI AGENT SOCIAL NETWORK
articles.summary: Meta has acquired the Reddit-esque simulated social network, Moltbook. Moltbook's creator, Matt Schlicht, and his business partner, Ben Parr, will work within Meta Superintelligence Labs. Moltbook was created using OpenClaw, a wrapper for LLM coding agents that lets users prompt them via popular chat apps. OpenClaw's founder was hired by OpenAI in February.

articles.title: YOUTUBE LAYS CLAIM TO ANOTHER CROWN: THE WORLD'S LARGEST MEDIA COMPANY
articles.summary: YouTube has become the world's largest media company. It gained more than $60 billion in revenue in 2025. The company is now valued between $500 billion and $560 billion, far above any traditional media competitors. YouTube's scale as a distributor, along with its heavy investment in AI tools, will help it continue its explosive growth.

articles.title: SPACEX'S STARSHIP MOON LANDER LIKELY TO FACE MORE DELAYS, REPORT SAYS
articles.summary: SpaceX's Starship lunar lander for NASA faces more delays. NASA recently moved the company's June 2027 moon landing deadline to 2028. A major review of Starship's design was postponed until August, leaving little room to meet the current schedule. The biggest challenge facing Starship's development is demonstrating the vehicle's ability to be refueled in orbit, which must be done repeatedly in order to reach the Moon.

articles.title: ONE VACCINE MAY PROVIDE BROAD PROTECTION AGAINST MANY RESPIRATORY INFECTIONS AND ALLERGENS
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a universal vaccine formula that protects against a wide range of respiratory viruses, bacteria, and even allergens. The vaccine is delivered intranasally, and it provides broad protection in the lungs for several months. It works by mimicking the signals that immune cells use to communicate with each other during an infection. The vaccine has only been tested in mice, but researchers estimate, in the best-case scenario, that it might be available in five to seven years.

articles.title: they need to generate code that's ready for git push. Unblocked
articles.summary: synthesizes your code, PR history, conversations, docs, and runtime signals to surface the right context for each task. See how it works.

articles.title: CRAWL ENTIRE WEBSITES WITH A SINGLE API CALL USING BROWSER RENDERING
articles.summary: Cloudflare's Browser Rendering API renders pages in a headless browser and returns the data in multiple formats, including HTML, Markdown, and structured JSON. It is now available in open beta for both the Workers Free and Paid plans. Users can configure crawl depth, page limits, and wildcard patterns to include or exclude specific URL paths. The crawler honors robots.txt directives.

articles.title: WHY I BELIEVE IN SOTA MODELS OVER CUSTOM ONES
articles.summary: The smarter and more experienced a human is, the better job they will do. Even small tasks benefit from the general life experience of the person doing the execution. This is why SOTA models will always be better than small, specialized models. The best models keep coming down in price, so enterprises will likely just use general models in the long term instead of tuning less capable models.

articles.title: WHY THE ATM DIDN'T KILL BANK TELLER JOBS, BUT THE IPHONE DID
articles.summary: ATMs were predicted to increase bank teller unemployment, but they didn't. However, the number of bank tellers started to fall after the birth of the iPhone. When a technology automates what a human does within an existing paradigm, it rarely gets rid of the human, as the definition of the paradigm creates the need for human involvement. The iPhone created a new paradigm that made the tasks that the ATM substituted irrelevant.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC TELLS JUDGE BILLIONS AT STAKE IF US SHUNS AI TOOL
articles.summary: Anthropic could lose billions of dollars in revenue this year due to the Department of War's declaration of the company as a supply-chain risk. It has asked a judge to take quick action on its request to block the decision. The declaration has led to more than 100 enterprise customers contacting Anthropic to express doubt about continuing their relationship. A hearing on Anthropic's request had been set for April 3, but the judge moved it up to March 24.

articles.title: AI SHOULD HELP US PRODUCE BETTER CODE
articles.summary: Quality enhancements that used to be time-consuming have now dropped in cost to the point where there's no excuse not to invest in quality while shipping new features.

articles.title: AMAZON CONVENES ‘DEEP DIVE' INTERNAL MEETING TO ADDRESS OUTAGES
articles.summary: An Amazon spokesperson says that a single incident was related to AI, and none of the incidents involved AI-written code.

articles.title: FROM EXPERIMENTAL TOOL TO AI INFRASTRUCTURE: ADAM ZIELIŃSKI'S VISION FOR WORDPRESS PLAYGROUND IN 2026
articles.summary: Zieliński's goal for 2026 is to make Playground useful and easily accessible for a large crowd out there that's not super technical.

articles.title: A UNIX MANIFESTO FOR THE AGE OF AI
articles.summary: The Unix philosophy has survived because its authors built restraint into the architecture.

articles.title: I BUILT A PROGRAMMING LANGUAGE USING CLAUDE CODE
articles.summary: Cutlet is a dynamic programming language built entirely by Claude Code that combines the expressiveness of Python, Ruby, Lua, and JavaScript with first-class support for running subprocesses, building pipelines, and scripting.
